title: EFT: Anxiety Split
description: Three practical clinician-facing slides extracted from the EFT interventions practice deck.

sources.label: Vrana and Greenberg, Overview of Emotion-Focused Therapy

slides.title: Marker: one part interrupts experience
slides.body: Treat anxiety as an active organizer with a feared consequence.
bullets: Name the interrupter's action: tighten, scan, silence, flee, freeze.
Ask what would happen if the emotion were allowed.
Look for the protected primary emotion underneath.

slides.title: Work with the protective logic
slides.body: Do not attack the anxiety. Make its protective function explicit, then help it update through contact with the living self.
steps: Invite the anxious interrupter to speak: what are you preventing?
Heighten its prediction: rejection, collapse, danger, humiliation.
Move to the interrupted self: what is blocked and what does it cost?
Facilitate dialogue until fear and need can both be represented.
Test a small permission: one breath, one sentence, one felt impulse.

slides.title: Resolution signals
slides.body: The task is resolving when regulation is no longer purchased by self-erasure.
items: The anxious side can say what it fears without escalating.
The interrupted side can express a need, protest, or grief.
The client can sense anxiety as protective rather than identical with self.
A new boundary or permission becomes behaviorally imaginable.
